--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://c.files.bbci.co.uk/11CAE/production/_114887827_coronavirus_global_area_chart_new_jh13oct-nc.png
DESCRIPTION: A graph illustrates the rapid increase in global coronavirus cases, showing a steep ascent from 30 million to 35 million within just 17 days, alongside the total death toll.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://www.tmc.edu/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/d-covid-19-positive-case-growth-trend-9-18-2020.png
DESCRIPTION: The graph illustrates the daily increase in COVID-19 positive cases over several months in the Greater Houston Area, highlighting trends and monitoring thresholds as of September 17, 2020.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://cdn.statcdn.com/Infographic/images/normal/25478.jpeg
DESCRIPTION: A graph shows the cumulative confirmed COVID-19 cases worldwide surpassing 200 million, with distinct color-coded segments representing cases in the United States, India, Brazil, Russia, France, and the rest of the world, illustrating a significant upward trend from 2020 to August 2021.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://static01.nyt.com/newsgraphics/2021/coronavirus-tracking/images/maps/NYT-World/cases.png
DESCRIPTION: A world map displays varying sizes of red circles representing COVID-19 deaths per capita, with notable concentrations in Europe and specific regions of Asia.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://d3i6fh83elv35t.cloudfront.net/static/2020/03/Screen-Shot-2020-03-05-at-6.29.29-PM-1024x574.png
DESCRIPTION: A world map illustrating the rapid global spread of COVID-19 cases by region, with circles of varying sizes representing the number of cases, notably showing significant outbreaks in Asia, Europe, and the United States as of March 5, 2020.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: The COVID-19 pandemic demonstrated unprecedented speed, with cases surging from late January 2020, affecting over 109 countries within weeks, illustrating our interconnectedness.
Countries implemented varied public health strategies, including lockdowns, mask mandates, and social distancing, aiming to mitigate transmission and protect healthcare systems.
The pandemic highlighted the need for robust surveillance, rapid response systems, and global cooperation in addressing emerging infectious diseases to prevent future outbreaks.
